title: alstroemeria nivea
intro: Alstroemeria nivea, also known as the White Peruvian Lily or White Flor de Mayo, is a species of flowering plant in the family Alstroemeriaceae. This plant is native to the Andean regions of South America, particularly in Peru and Chile. Alstroemeria nivea is known for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are often used to treat wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be used to make herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and salves, which are said to offer health benefits including reduced inflammation, improved digestion, and enhanced immune function.
